tidyfault 0.0.0.9
  • Reference

    illustrate() Function

    illustrate.Rd

    This function takes nodes and edges from a fault tree, computes a tree layout using tidygraph and ggraph, and returns ggplot-friendly data.frames of nodes, edges, and gate polygons.

    illustrate(
  nodes,
  edges,
  type = c("nodes", "edges", "both", "all"),
  node_key = "id",
  layout = "tree",
  size = 0.25,
  scale_size = FALSE,
  res = 50
)

    Arguments

    nodes

    (Required) dataset of nodes, including an id field (or whatever is written in node_key)

    edges

    (Required) dataset of edges, including from and to fields corresponding to the id of each node

    type

    (Optional) Character string specifying output format. Default is "both", which returns a list with "nodes", "edges", and "gates". Other options: "nodes" returns only node coordinates, "edges" returns only edge coordinates, "all" returns nodes, edges, gates, and pairwise edge data.

    node_key

    (Optional) Character string naming the column in nodes that contains unique node identifiers. Default is "id".

    layout

    (Optional) Character string specifying the graph layout algorithm. Default is "tree". "dendrogram" also works well. See ggraph::create_layout() for more layout options (e.g., "fr", "kk", "nicely").

    size

    (Optional) Numeric value passed to gate() function for constructing polygons. Controls the diameter of gate shapes. Defaults to 0.25. When scale_size = TRUE, this is scaled by the layout extent.

    scale_size

    (Optional) Logical. If TRUE, scale gate size to the layout extent. Default is FALSE; set to TRUE to try consistent proportions across very different trees.

    res

    (Optional) Numeric value passed to gate() function for constructing polygons. Controls the number of line segments used to draw curved gate shapes. Defaults to 50.

    Value

    The return type depends on the type parameter:

    • For type = "nodes": A data.frame with node coordinates (x, y) and all original columns from nodes

    • For type = "edges": A data.frame with edge coordinates formatted for geom_line(), containing columns direction, id, x, y, edge_id

    • For type = "both" (default): A named list with three elements:

      • nodes: Node coordinates data.frame

      • edges: Edge coordinates data.frame

      • gates: Gate polygon coordinates data.frame with x, y columns for drawing gate shapes

    • For type = "all": A named list with four elements: nodes, edges, gates, and pairwise (edge data with from/to coordinates)

    Details

    This function prepares fault tree data for visualization with ggplot2 by:

    • Creating a tidygraph object from nodes and edges

    • Computing graph layout coordinates using ggraph::create_layout() with the specified layout algorithm

    • Extracting node coordinates and preserving original node attributes

    • Computing edge coordinates by joining node positions to edge endpoints

    • Generating gate polygon shapes for AND, OR, and top event gates using the gate() function

    The output is designed to work seamlessly with ggplot2. Nodes can be plotted with geom_point(), edges with geom_line(), and gates with geom_polygon(). The tree layout algorithm positions nodes hierarchically, making fault tree structure easy to visualize.

    See also

    gate for generating gate polygons, tbl_graph for creating graph objects, create_layout for layout algorithms

    Contents

    Developed by Timothy Fraser, Jingyao Tong.
